tools.name: search_products
tools.description: Search the allover.art catalog (one card per design). Args: query? (free text, e.g. 'cat', 'neon koi'), theme? (canonical theme like Cats/Anime/Skulls, comma-separated for several), section? (mens|womens|kids), limit? (<=48), max_price? (USD). Returns product cards with good_id, price and url.

tools.name: get_product
tools.description: Full details of one product by its code: available sizes, fabrics, per-variant price, and the design 'about' text. Args: code (from search_products).

tools.name: list_collections
tools.description: List active curated collections (themed landing pages) with their slugs and urls. Args: limit?.

tools.name: view_cart
tools.description: Show the current contents of the user's cart (the same cart they see in the browser when signed in).

tools.name: add_to_cart
tools.description: Add a product to the user's cart. Args: good_id (from search/get_product), size (must be one of the product's sizes), fabric? (if the product offers a choice), qty? (default 1). Returns the updated cart.

tools.name: set_cart_quantity
tools.description: Change the quantity of a cart line by its index (from view_cart); qty=0 removes it. Args: index, qty.

tools.name: checkout_link
tools.description: Get the URL where the human opens their cart to review and pay. The agent never pays — checkout is always done by the person in the browser.

tools.name: order_status
tools.description: Look up the user's recent orders (or one by number) and their fulfilment status. Args: number?, limit? (default 5).

tools.name: cart_update
tools.description: Change a cart line by its index (from view_cart): set qty (0 removes), or switch size/fabric. Args: index, qty?, size?, fabric?.

tools.name: cart_remove
tools.description: Remove a cart line by its index (from view_cart). Args: index.

tools.name: apply_promo
tools.description: Validate and apply a promo code to the cart (server-checked). Returns the new discount/shipping/total, or applied=false with a reason. Args: code.

tools.name: remove_promo
tools.description: Remove the applied promo code from the cart.

tools.name: get_saved_addresses
tools.description: Return shipping addresses the user used in past orders — offer these to a returning buyer instead of asking again.

tools.name: set_shipping_address
tools.description: Set the shipping address for this order (validated). Args: name, country, city, address, zip?, phone.

tools.name: get_shipping_options
tools.description: Available shipping methods for the current cart/address, with price and ETA (free over the threshold).

tools.name: set_shipping_method
tools.description: Choose a shipping method by its option_id (from get_shipping_options). Args: option_id.

tools.name: get_order_preview
tools.description: Full order summary to show the human before ordering: line items, promo/discount, shipping, address, grand total. Prices are server-computed.

tools.name: prepare_order
tools.description: Create a draft order from the cart + address + promo and return {checkout_url, draft_id, expires_at}. The human opens checkout_url — everything is pre-filled — reviews and pays. The agent never pays. Call get_order_preview and confirm with the human first.

tools.name: studio_options
tools.description: START HERE for any custom-print request: the menu of generation choices (print placements with plain-language descriptions, products, backgrounds, engines+costs). Present these to the human — at minimum ask which PLACEMENT and which PRODUCT they want — before calling studio_generate.

tools.name: studio_generate
tools.description: Generate a CUSTOM t-shirt print from a text prompt (AI, takes 2-10 min, costs the user's Studio credits — a free starter balance is auto-granted on first use). IMPORTANT: first call studio_options and ask the human to pick placement + product (and optionally background); do NOT silently use defaults

tools.name: studio_status
tools.description: Check a custom print generation. Args: print_id (from studio_generate). When done returns preview_url — show it to the human.

tools.name: studio_credits
tools.description: The user's Studio credit balance. Check before studio_generate (1 credit per generation, either engine; welcome bonus 20; every bought tee adds 20).

tools.name: add_custom_to_cart
tools.description: Put a FINISHED custom print into the cart as a product. Args: print_id (must be done), product? (same set as studio_generate), size, qty?. Then the normal checkout flow applies (get_order_preview -> prepare_order).

tools.name: designer_stats
tools.description: The user's designer-program stats (if they are a designer-partner): units sold, revenue, accrued royalties, available for payout, royalty percent, their designer page URL.

tools.name: designer_designs
tools.description: The user's own published designs with live status (products on sale / hidden / pending). Args: limit? (default 50).

tools.name: designer_submissions
tools.description: The user's Studio publications in moderation: in review / in catalog / needs changes. Args: limit?.

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "checkout_link" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Get the URL where the human opens their cart to review and pay. The agent never pays — checkout is always done by the person in the browser.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "studio_options" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: START HERE for any custom-print request: the menu of generation choices (print placements with plain-language descriptions, products, backgrounds, engines+costs…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "studio_generate" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

evidence.snippet: Generate a CUSTOM t-shirt print from a text prompt (AI, takes 2-10 min, costs the user's Studio credits — a free starter balance is auto-granted on first use). …

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "studio_generate" is 1249 chars, far longer than this server's median (127). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Generate a CUSTOM t-shirt print from a text prompt (AI, takes 2-10 min, costs the user's Studio credits — a free starter balance is auto-granted on first use). …
